title: nvidia-nemo/labs-molt
summary: A high-performance, PyTorch-native framework for agentic reinforcement learning, designed for scaling to frontier-class models using Ray, vLLM, and NVIDIA AutoModel.
risk_notes: Requires significant compute resources (NVIDIA GPUs) and complex dependencies like Ray and vLLM. Installation typically involves pip-based workflows; requires careful management of environment variables and potentially sensitive API keys for LLM-as-a-judge or external tool integration. The project is highly specialized for research and requires deep knowledge of distributed training architectures.

title: katanemo/plano
summary: Plano is an AI-native proxy and data plane built on Envoy that provides orchestration, observability, and guardrails for agentic applications.
risk_notes: Distributed as a Docker image or binary. It acts as a central proxy for LLM traffic, meaning it requires access to sensitive API keys (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) and handles all incoming/outgoing agent traffic. It is actively maintained by core Envoy contributors, but requires careful configuration of network policies and secret management.

title: getzep/graphiti
summary: Graphiti is a powerful open-source framework for building temporal knowledge graphs, allowing AI agents to maintain evolving, context-aware memory of facts and relationships over time.
risk_notes: Installed via standard Python pip packages. It requires integration with LLM providers (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic) and likely a vector database, necessitating API keys. It is actively maintained by a commercial entity (Zep) and is designed for integration into production agent pipelines, which requires careful management of data privacy and access control for the ingested context.

title: insforge/insforge
summary: InsForge is an all-in-one backend platform designed specifically for AI coding agents, providing integrated database, auth, storage, and compute capabilities via an MCP server or CLI.
risk_notes: The platform offers both a cloud-hosted version and a self-hosted option. As an agentic backend, it requires significant access to your infrastructure, including database credentials, storage buckets, and API keys for LLM providers. Self-hosting involves managing complex infrastructure (Postgres, Deno, etc.), which increases the attack surface if not properly secured.

title: shepherd-agents/shepherd
summary: A runtime substrate for AI agents that provides reversible execution traces, sandboxing, and copy-on-write workspaces to safely supervise and optimize agent behavior.
risk_notes: Installed via pip. Uses OS-level sandboxing (Seatbelt on macOS, Landlock on Linux) to restrict agent access. Requires API keys (e.g., Anthropic) for agent functionality, though it supports offline/keyless modes. The project is in early alpha, so APIs and security implementations are subject to change.

title: agrinman/tunnelto
summary: A high-performance, Rust-based CLI tool for exposing local web servers to the internet via secure tunnels, with support for self-hosting the server component.
risk_notes: Installs via cargo or brew. Requires network access to tunnel traffic from the public internet to your local machine. Self-hosting requires deploying a server component, which involves managing TCP/websocket ports. The project has not seen significant updates since 2022, suggesting it may be in maintenance mode.

title: atarity/deploy-your-own-saas
summary: A comprehensive, curated list of self-hostable alternatives to popular proprietary cloud services, categorized by function.
risk_notes: This is a directory, not a tool itself. The individual projects listed vary wildly in security posture and installation methods, ranging from simple Docker containers to complex Ansible playbooks. Users must evaluate each linked project's security, maintenance, and resource requirements individually before deployment.

title: getclawe/clawe
summary: A multi-agent coordination system that uses a Kanban-style interface to manage AI agents, allowing them to collaborate on tasks via a Convex backend and Docker-based infrastructure.
risk_notes: Requires Docker/Docker Compose for deployment and a Convex account for backend state management. It necessitates providing Anthropic/OpenAI API keys, which the agents will use to perform tasks. The system runs agents in isolated workspaces, but these agents have access to local files and the network to perform their duties. The project is very new (created Feb 2026), so it should be treated as experimental.

title: adminsyspro/proxcenter-ui
summary: ProxCenter is a comprehensive management platform for Proxmox VE and Proxmox Backup Server, offering multi-cluster orchestration, migration tools, and monitoring in a unified interface.
risk_notes: Installation uses a 'curl | sudo bash' script, which requires high-level system privileges. The tool requires administrative access to your Proxmox clusters to perform management tasks, meaning it holds significant control over your virtualized infrastructure. It is actively maintained with security scanning integrations (CodeQL, SonarCloud).

title: serial-studio/serial-studio
summary: A powerful, cross-platform telemetry dashboard for visualizing and interacting with hardware devices via serial, network, and industrial protocols.
risk_notes: Distributed as precompiled binaries for major OSs. Requires standard user permissions to access serial ports and network interfaces. The application supports scripting (JS/Lua) and external API integrations, which should be handled with caution if loading untrusted project files. Actively maintained with frequent updates.

title: winapps-org/winapps
summary: A powerful tool for running Windows applications seamlessly on Linux via RDP and virtualization (Docker/Libvirt/Podman), enabling native-like integration for apps like Microsoft Office and Adobe Creative Cloud.
risk_notes: Requires virtualization (KVM/Libvirt or Docker/Podman) and FreeRDP. It mounts the host's home directory into the Windows VM, which grants the Windows environment significant access to your Linux files. It is a complex setup involving system-level virtualization, so it requires careful configuration of the VM's security posture.

title: spacehuhntech/wifiduck
summary: A wireless keystroke injection platform that allows for remote execution of Ducky Script payloads via a web interface on ESP8266/Atmega32u4 hardware.
risk_notes: This is a hardware-based security tool designed for keystroke injection. It requires flashing firmware onto microcontrollers using the Arduino IDE. It operates as a HID device, meaning it can bypass standard OS security by mimicking a keyboard. It is intended for authorized security testing and educational purposes only.

title: safishamsi/graphify
summary: A powerful CLI tool that generates interactive knowledge graphs from local codebases and documentation, allowing for semantic querying of project structure without relying solely on vector embeddings.
risk_notes: Installed via 'uv' or 'pipx', which is a safe, isolated installation method. The tool requires read access to your local filesystem to parse code and documentation. It may require external API keys (e.g., OpenAI/Claude) if you choose to process non-code assets like images or PDFs via LLM, but core code parsing is local and deterministic.

title: pucherot/pi.alert
summary: A network monitoring and intrusion detection tool designed for home labs and Raspberry Pi environments that tracks connected devices and alerts on unauthorized access.
risk_notes: Installation uses a curl-to-bash script which requires root privileges to configure system services, web servers, and network scanning utilities. It requires access to network traffic and DHCP/DNS logs to function, which is standard for an IDS, but users should be aware of the broad system-level permissions granted during installation.

title: gobackup/gobackup
summary: A robust, dependency-free CLI tool for automating database and file backups to various cloud storage providers with built-in scheduling and web UI management.
risk_notes: Installation via 'curl | sh' is provided, which carries inherent security risks; manual binary installation or Homebrew is preferred. The tool requires access to database credentials and cloud storage API keys to function. It runs as a daemon and requires filesystem read access to the databases and files being backed up.

title: ramonvermeulen/whosthere
summary: A high-performance, unprivileged LAN discovery tool written in Go that provides both an interactive TUI and a daemon mode with an HTTP API.
risk_notes: Installs via standard package managers (brew, nix, yay) or Go. It runs in user-space without requiring root/sudo privileges. It performs network scanning (mDNS, SSDP, ARP) and optional port scanning, which is standard for network diagnostic tools. The project is actively maintained and written in memory-safe Go.

title: iamtomshaw/f1-race-replay
summary: An interactive Python-based visualization tool for Formula 1 race telemetry and event replays using the FastF1 library.
risk_notes: Standard Python project installation via pip. Requires network access to fetch race data from F1 APIs via the FastF1 library. No sensitive credentials required, but it creates a local cache directory (.fastf1-cache) in the project root. Maintenance is active, though it has a high number of open issues.

title: potatameister/paperknife
summary: A privacy-focused, zero-server PDF utility that performs all operations locally in the browser or via a Capacitor-based Android app using WebAssembly.
risk_notes: The tool is highly secure as it runs entirely client-side (browser or local app) with no server-side processing. It requires no network access to function, though it does require file system access to read/write the PDFs you choose to process. It is actively maintained and distributed via standard web hosting or APK/F-Droid.

title: tablecruncher/tablecruncher
summary: A high-performance C++ CSV editor capable of handling massive files with built-in JavaScript macro support.
risk_notes: Distributed as prebuilt binaries for major OSs or buildable via CMake/FLTK. As a local desktop application, it has standard user-level filesystem access. It includes a JavaScript engine (Duktape) for macros, which should be treated with caution if running untrusted CSV files or scripts. Maintenance is active following its recent transition to open source.

title: ilya-zlobintsev/lact
summary: LACT is a powerful Rust-based utility for monitoring and configuring AMD, Nvidia, and Intel GPUs on Linux, featuring a system daemon for headless control and a GTK4 GUI.
risk_notes: Requires root privileges via a systemd service (lactd) to interact with hardware interfaces. It manages GPU power and clock states, which carries inherent hardware risk if misconfigured. Installation is handled via standard package managers (pacman, apt, dnf) or Flatpak, which is safer than script-based installs. The daemon uses Unix socket permissions to restrict access to specific user groups.

title: jjang-ai/mlxstudio
summary: A native macOS desktop application and CLI tool for running local LLMs and image generation models using Apple's MLX framework, featuring high-performance inference and an OpenAI-compatible API server.
risk_notes: The project provides a signed/notarized DMG installer for macOS, which is a standard and safe installation method. The CLI version can be installed via pipx or uv, which isolates dependencies. As an AI inference tool, it requires local filesystem access to download and store model weights (which can be large) and network access to fetch models from HuggingFace. It runs a local API server on localhost:8000, which is standard for local LLM tools.

title: microck/ordinary-claude-skills
summary: A comprehensive, community-aggregated collection of Claude Skills and prompt-based tools organized by category for use with MCP servers or direct Claude integration.
risk_notes: The repository is a collection of text-based prompt files and scripts. It requires cloning the repository and potentially using the @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem to expose these files to an LLM. Users should review individual scripts before execution, as they may contain arbitrary code or instructions that could interact with local files or external APIs depending on the specific skill used.

title: khcrysalis/impactor
summary: A cross-platform, Rust-based iOS sideloading tool that simplifies signing and installing IPA files, managing certificates, and injecting tweaks.
risk_notes: Requires Apple ID credentials to function as a developer account for signing. Installs via Homebrew or Flatpak. Interacts with system-level USB drivers (usbmuxd on Linux, iTunes drivers on Windows) and requires local filesystem access to store signing keys and certificates. Active maintenance, but requires caution as it handles sensitive authentication tokens.

title: confident-ai/deepteam
summary: DeepTeam is a comprehensive, local-first framework for red teaming LLM applications, offering automated testing for vulnerabilities like prompt injection, PII leakage, and various security bypasses.
risk_notes: Installed via pip. Requires API keys for the LLMs used during evaluation (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic). It runs locally, but by design, it performs adversarial probing against your own AI agents, which requires careful configuration to avoid unintended side effects on production systems. The project is actively maintained and built on the established DeepEval framework.

title: ai4finance-foundation/finrl-trading
summary: FinRL-X is a modular, AI-native quantitative trading framework designed for building, backtesting, and executing algorithmic trading strategies using reinforcement learning and traditional finance models.
risk_notes: Requires Python environment setup via pip. The application handles sensitive financial API keys (e.g., Alpaca) and performs live market execution, which carries significant financial risk. It requires network access to market data providers and brokerage APIs. The codebase is actively maintained and uses standard Pydantic configurations for security.
